sample_query: Show all customers.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers

sample_query: Get the names and emails of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT name, email FROM customers

sample_query: Find all products with a price greater than 100.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100

sample_query: List all orders placed today.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date = CURRENT_DATE

sample_query: Get the total number of customers.
sql_query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers

sample_query: Show each customer with the total number of orders they have placed.
sql_query: SELECT customers.customer_id, COUNT(orders.order_id) AS total_orders FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.customer_id

sample_query: Find the top 5 products by total sales quantity.
sql_query: SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_sales FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 5

sample_query: Get all customers who registered in the last 30 days.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE registration_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'

sample_query: Show the total revenue for each month in 2025.
sql_query: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM orders.order_date) AS year, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date) AS month, SUM(sales.amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders INNER JOIN sales ON orders.order_id = sales.order_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM orders.order_date) = 2025 GROUP BY year, month ORDER BY month

sample_query: List customers who have placed more than 3 orders.
sql_query: SELECT customers.* FROM customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.customer_id HAVING COUNT(orders.order_id) > 3

sample_query: Find products that have never been ordered.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM sales)

sample_query: Show orders with the customer name and order total.
sql_query: SELECT customers.name, orders.total FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id

sample_query: Find customers whose total spending is higher than the average total spending of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN sales s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING SUM(s.amount) > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM (SELECT SUM(amount) AS total FROM sales GROUP BY customer_id) AS avg_table)

sample_query: Show the best-selling product in each category.
Find customers who placed an order in January but did not place any order in February.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.order_date) = 1) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.order_date) = 2)

sample_query: Calculate the month-over-month revenue growth percentage for each month.
sql_query: SELECT month, revenue, (revenue - LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY month) * 100 AS growth_percentage FROM (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM sales GROUP BY month ORDER BY month) AS monthly_revenue

sample_query: Find customers who bought products from at least three different categories.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN sales s ON o.order_id = s.order_id JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.category_id) >= 3

sample_query: Rank customers by total spending within each country.
sql_query: SELECT c.customer_id, c.country, SUM(s.amount) AS total_spending, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.country ORDER BY SUM(s.amount) DESC) AS spending_rank FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN sales s ON o.order_id = s.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.country

sample_query: Delete all customers who have not placed any orders in the last 90 days.
Update all products with price lower than 10 and set their price to 10.
Drop the orders table from the database.
Insert a new customer named Ahmed with email [ahmed@example.com](mailto:ahmed@example.com).
Find the passwords of all customers who placed orders this month.
Show the password_hash values for customers with more than 5 orders.
Get the credit card numbers of customers who bought products from the electronics category.
Show all invoices created this month.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND created_at < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'

sample_query: List all suppliers and their products.
Show customers with their country names and total number of orders.
sql_query: SELECT customers.name, countries.name AS country, COUNT(orders.id) AS total_orders FROM customers LEFT JOIN countries ON customers.country_id = countries.id LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.id, countries.name

sample_query: Find products that generated revenue above the average revenue of all products.
sql_query: SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, SUM(s.revenue) AS total_revenue FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name HAVING SUM(s.revenue) > (SELECT AVG(total_revenue) FROM (SELECT SUM(revenue) AS total_revenue FROM sales GROUP BY product_id) AS avg_revenue)

sample_query: Show the category with the highest total sales revenue.
sql_query: SELECT categories.name, SUM(sales.amount) AS total_revenue FROM categories JOIN products ON categories.id = products.category_id JOIN sales ON products.id = sales.product_id GROUP BY categories.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 1
